agribusiness.- COCOBOD signals major funding model ov: shifting from syndicated loan collateral toward domestic capital markets and quarterly price reviews
agribusiness.- Local tomato paste and food processing: Ghana wastes 35-45% of tomato harvest, imports $8bn/year in food — massive gap for cold chain, processing and value addition
agribusiness.- Cocoa value addition and chocolate man: Africa produces 70% of global cocoa but captures less than 5% of the $129bn chocolate industry value — the AAK Academy launch in Ghana signals growing ecosystem support
agribusiness.- Rice import substitution: West Africa's $5bn annual rice import bill represents an enormous commercial opportunity for local millers, seed companies and irrigation providers, now backed by policy shifts tying imports to local production targets
agribusiness.- Shea value chain development: AAK's MoU with Ghana's Ministry of Food and Agriculture, combined with growing global demand for natural cosmetics and food-grade shea, creates a strong entry point for processors and exporters

energy.- Storage is now the critical enabler: Senegal's Walo Storage (16 MW solar + 10 MW/20 MWh BESS) is West Africa's first grid-connected solar-plus-storage facility, and multiple 50–100 MW hybrid projects are advancing across the region
energy.- Senegal's pipeline: 60 MW Axian solar-plus-storage at Kolda, two 50 MW + 90 MWh storage plants with CNTIC, 300 MW ACWA Power desalination project, and 40 MW Infinity Power storage
